key_insights.insight: Pursuing mastery requires intense focus, but also openness to inspiration and collaboration with others outside your bubble.
key_insights.quote: The focus it takes is is pretty intense... and also I think that the ability to listen and to take cues or inspiration from others around you.

key_insights.insight: Early access to money and fame can derail motivation if the initial passion for the craft wasn't deeply rooted.
key_insights.quote: If they get any sense of fame or fortune, they lose their motivation. So I agree with you in that sense.

key_insights.insight: Experiencing an activity's commercial downturn can refocus the passion and prevent burnout.
key_insights.quote: Luckily I had been doing it for so long at that point and had seen it come and go that I was excited in the sense that skateboarding was going to get a new, arenewed interest. And if I was the conduit to that, then I'll accept it.

key_insights.insight: Burnout can stem from the pressure of success, but learning to let go and detach from the identity of being the best can reignite passion.
key_insights.quote: What it did teach me was the value of letting go. And when I let go of that...I started to appreciate the process of learning new tricks more...And then when I came back to competing, I let go of the idea of perfection.

key_insights.insight: The fear of intimacy and vulnerability can drive compulsive behaviors, and confronting these fears can lead to more fulfilling relationships.
key_insights.quote: Yeah, I think a lot of it was just being afraid of intimacy...I had to figure that out and, and, and, and how, well, how to be vulnerable.

key_insights.insight: Integrating work on your mental state and relationship with your priorities alongside your career or passion improves overall well-being and relationships.
key_insights.quote: I think I would have told my younger self to work on work on your state of mind and your priorities with, with equal effort as you do your skating. I was so hyper fixated on my skating and getting better and my success in skating that, um, I didn't really work on my humanity.

key_insights.insight: It's important to find a partner who is grounded and has their own priorities intact, providing a sense of home and perspective, shielding one from excessive fanfare.
key_insights.quote: She's just so grounded and she gives me a sense of home and she is very supportive, but also has her priorities intact...she's not swayed by fanfare at all.

key_insights.insight: Embracing mistakes and learning from failures are essential for personal evolution and growth.
key_insights.quote: I've always learned to embrace my mistakes with skateboarding. And in a sense, I do that with my regular life too but they embraced that the idea that i grew from them.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are losing your way in terms of priorities, seek feedback from trusted partners or friends.
actionable_advice.how_to: Actively ask for input on your behavior and priorities, and be open to hearing constructive criticism.

actionable_advice.advice: Challenge yourself to step outside your comfort zone in your field of expertise.
actionable_advice.how_to: Try a new technique, collaborate with someone outside your usual circle, or explore a different style.

actionable_advice.advice: If you find yourself being disconnected from the reason you started your career or passion seek help to figure out how to process it and move forward.
actionable_advice.how_to: Engage in therapy or counseling to make progress

memorable_stories: Tony's story of hiding his skateboard in the bushes in high school due to it not being 'cool'.
The story of Tony's dad, who wasn't paid, creating the National Skateboard Association.
The moment of landing the 900 after 12 years and multiple injuries, and realizing the weight shift needed to succeed.
How Tony emailed Neversoft to include the 900 trick in the game and they responded 'way ahead of you, you fucking rule'.
The story of Tony almost taking a $500k buyout before the Tony Hawk Pro Skater game released. He decided to bet on himself and receive royalties from the game.
contrarian_claims: The idea that being paid for something that was once a hobby declines your love and motivation.
The judges at events were giving him scores based on what they thought I could do and not compared to everyone else in the event.
The term bravery actually means sharing your feelings.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Tony Hawk: The Man With The $1.4 Billion Name! Burnout, Obsession & Regrets
